title: Clarify runtime-to-ticket status synchronization expectations
summary: Define and document how runtime transaction status and persisted ticket-document status must stay synchronized during execution. The goal is to remove ambiguity about whether runtime-only status changes are sufficient and to ensure that per-ticket JSON files plus `tickets_list.json` reflect the same lifecycle progression expected by the canonical work loop.
outputs: Documented synchronization expectations between runtime transaction state and ticket-document status
Clarified work-loop guidance for when persisted ticket status must change
Implementation guidance for keeping per-ticket JSON and `tickets_list.json` aligned with runtime execution
acceptance_criteria: The ticket defines how runtime transaction status/phase maps to persisted ticket-document status values.
The ticket defines that persisted ticket status must remain synchronized with runtime execution throughout the work loop, not only at planning start or final completion.
The ticket clarifies how per-ticket JSON files and `tickets_list.json` should be updated together when status changes.
The synchronization guidance is specific enough to prevent divergent interpretations about whether runtime-only updates are sufficient.
The ticket scope remains focused on synchronization expectations and does not introduce a separate docs-only status model.
tasks: Review the current work-loop wording and identify where runtime-to-ticket status synchronization is implied but not explicit.
Define the expected mapping between runtime transaction lifecycle states and persisted ticket-document status values.
Define when both the per-ticket JSON file and `tickets_list.json` must be updated during the canonical work loop.
Clarify how synchronization should behave for intermediate states such as `in-progress`, `checking`, `verified`, and `committed`.
Document the synchronization expectations in a form that can guide later rules, implementation, and regression-test updates.
notes: This ticket is about synchronization expectations and guidance, not about changing the status enum itself.
Do not define a separate status lifecycle for docs that can drift from runtime transaction state.
Keep the contract strict enough that operators and resume logic can trust versioned planning artifacts as a reflection of execution progress.
Coordinate closely with the rules/template alignment work so persistence destinations and synchronization timing remain consistent.
